title: Ptarmica schouwii
intro: Ptarmica schouwii, also known as the Mountain Snorkel or Snowy Anemone, is a perennial plant species native to mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, fever, and inflammation, due to its health benefits in reducing pain and promoting relaxation. The bioactive constituents of Ptarmica schouwii include sesquiterpene lactones, such as heliantholides and inulin, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ptarmica schouwii include tinctures, infusions, and dried roots, often used in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Ptarmica schouwii is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae), native to the Mediterranean region. It is a member of the genus Ptarmica, which is closely related to the genus Achillea. Ptarmica schouwii is further classified within the subgenus Ptarmica, and its classification is as follows: Ptarmica schouwii (Schouw) Besser ex Rchb. f. subsp. schouwii. This species is often included in the larger genus Achillea, where it is considered a synonym of Achillea schouwii.
